    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom)

/-- **Uniqueness of a natural transformation between right adjoints from its unit transpose.** Two
natural transformations `Œ≤ Œ≥ : R‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ R‚ÇÇ` between the right adjoints of `adj‚ÇÅ : L‚ÇÅ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÅ`,
`adj‚ÇÇ : L‚ÇÇ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÇ` that have the same composite with the unit against a fixed `Œ± : L‚ÇÇ ‚ü∂ L‚ÇÅ`
(`adj‚ÇÅ.unit.app c ‚â´ -.app (L‚ÇÅ.obj c) = adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app c ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app c)` for all `c`) are equal.
This is the uniqueness underlying the adjoint-mate bijection `conjugateEquiv`: each side is forced to
the explicit triangle-identity formula `adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y) ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y)) ‚â´
R‚ÇÇ.map (adj‚ÇÅ.counit.app Y)`. Reusable for any `conjugateEquiv`-value identification (it powers both
the algebraic and geometric `b2` mate legs). Project-local. -/
lemma natTrans_ext_of_unit {C D : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C] [CategoryTheory.Category D]
    {L‚ÇÅ L‚ÇÇ : CategoryTheory.Functor C D} {R‚ÇÅ R‚ÇÇ : CategoryTheory.Functor D C}
    (adj‚ÇÅ : L‚ÇÅ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÅ) (adj‚ÇÇ : L‚ÇÇ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÇ) (Œ± : L‚ÇÇ ‚ü∂ L‚ÇÅ) (Œ≤ Œ≥ : R‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ R‚ÇÇ)
    (hŒ≤ : ‚àÄ c, adj‚ÇÅ.unit.app c ‚â´ Œ≤.app (L‚ÇÅ.obj c) = adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app c ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app c))
    (hŒ≥ : ‚àÄ c, adj‚ÇÅ.unit.app c ‚â´ Œ≥.app (L‚ÇÅ.obj c) = adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app c ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app c)) :
    Œ≤ = Œ≥ := by
  apply NatTrans.ext
  funext Y
  -- Each `Œ¥` satisfying the unit relation is pinned to the same explicit formula.
  have key : ‚àÄ (Œ¥ : R‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ R‚ÇÇ),
      (‚àÄ c, adj‚ÇÅ.unit.app c ‚â´ Œ¥.app (L‚ÇÅ.obj c) = adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app c ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app c)) ‚Üí
      Œ¥.app Y = adj‚ÇÇ.unit.app (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y) ‚â´ R‚ÇÇ.map (Œ±.app (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y)) ‚â´
        R‚ÇÇ.map (adj‚ÇÅ.counit.app Y) := by
    intro Œ¥ hŒ¥
    -- Insert the right-triangle identity `ùüô = unit ‚â´ R‚ÇÅ.map counit` (the surface form is
    -- defeq-obscured, so `simp` rather than a positional `rw`), then naturality of `Œ¥` and the unit
    -- relation `hŒ¥` pin `Œ¥.app Y` to the formula.
    have e1 : Œ¥.app Y
        = (adj‚ÇÅ.unit.app (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y) ‚â´ R‚ÇÅ.map (adj‚ÇÅ.counit.app Y)) ‚â´ Œ¥.app Y := by simp
    rw [e1, Category.assoc]
    simp [reassoc_of% (hŒ¥ (R‚ÇÅ.obj Y))]
  rw [key Œ≤ hŒ≤, key Œ≥ hŒ≥]

/-- **The extend-scalars composition coherence is the adjoint mate of the restrict-scalars
composition coherence.** For ring homs `f : R ‚Üí+* S`, `g : S ‚Üí+* T`, transporting the
`ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp f g` natural iso through the conjugate equivalence of the composite
extend‚Äìrestrict adjunction `(extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).comp (extendRestrictScalarsAdj g)` against
the single adjunction `extendRestrictScalarsAdj (g.comp f)` yields the inverse of
`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp f g`. The per-piece mate fact powering
`chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_extendScalarsComp`. Project-local. -/
lemma conjugateEquiv_extendScalarsComp {R S T : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [CommRing T]
    (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (g : S ‚Üí+* T) :
    conjugateEquiv
        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).comp (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj g))
        (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (g.comp f))
        (ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp f g).hom
      = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp f g).inv := by
  -- Both sides satisfy the SAME unit transpose against `Œ± = extendScalarsComp.hom`, so they agree by
  -- `natTrans_ext_of_unit`. For `conjugateEquiv` this is `unit_conjugateEquiv`; for
  -- `restrictScalarsComp.inv` it is `homEquiv_extendScalarsComp` (Mathlib's unit-side coherence),
  -- read off the composite-adjunction unit by `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`/`homEquiv_unit`.
  refine natTrans_ext_of_unit ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).comp
      (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj g)) (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (g.comp f))
      (ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp f g).hom _ _ (fun c => ?_) (fun c => ?_)
  ¬∑ -- Unit transpose of the conjugate is definitional (`unit_conjugateEquiv`).
    exact unit_conjugateEquiv _ _ _ c
  ¬∑ -- Unit transpose of `restrictScalarsComp.inv` is `homEquiv_extendScalarsComp`.
    have h := ModuleCat.homEquiv_extendScalarsComp f g c
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit] at h
    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app]
    -- `(A ‚â´ B) ‚â´ C = A ‚â´ B ‚â´ C` via term-mode `Category.assoc` (positional `rw` hits the diamond).
    exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans h.symm

/-- The conjugate (adjoint mate) of an `eqToHom` between extend-scalars functors is the `eqToHom`
between the corresponding restrict-scalars functors. The middle congruence piece of the algebraic leg
`chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_extendScalarsComp`. Project-local. -/
lemma conjugateEquiv_extendScalars_eqToHom {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    {f g : R ‚Üí+* S} (h : f = g) :
    conjugateEquiv (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj g) (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj f)
        (eqToHom (by rw [h] : ModuleCat.extendScalars f = ModuleCat.extendScalars g))
      = eqToHom (by rw [h] : ModuleCat.restrictScalars g = ModuleCat.restrictScalars f) := by
  subst h
  simp only [eqToHom_refl, conjugateEquiv_id]

/-- **(b2) Algebraic-leg coherence: the module reassociation through `extendScalarsComp`**
(blueprint `lem:chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_extendScalarsComp`). Under the algebraic
extend‚Äìrestrict adjunctions, the conjugate (adjoint mate) of the extend-scalars reassociation
`chartBaseChangeModuleReassocNat` is the matching restrict-scalars reassociation
`chartBaseChangeRestrictReassocNat`. Both are built from the composition coherences
(`ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp`/`restrictScalarsComp`) and the `eqToIso` of the same base-change
ring square. This is the algebraic leg of the ring-square mate-glue
`pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue`. Project-local. -/
theorem chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_extendScalarsComp {A R R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (œà : A ‚ü∂ B) (œÜ : A ‚ü∂ R) (œÅ : R ‚ü∂ R') :
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
    conjugateIsoEquiv
        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÅ.hom).comp
          (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (CommRingCat.ofHom
            (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
              (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u)))).hom))
        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (CommRingCat.ofHom
            (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
              (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u)))).hom).comp
          (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ).hom))
        (chartBaseChangeModuleReassocNat œà œÜ œÅ)
      = chartBaseChangeRestrictReassocNat œà œÜ œÅ := by
  -- ROUTE (iter-018, option (b)): mirror the geometric leg ‚Äî split the 3-factor conjugate with
  -- `conjugateEquiv_comp`, discharge the two `extendScalarsComp` factors by
  -- `conjugateEquiv_extendScalarsComp` (one inverted) and the middle `eqToHom` congruence by
  -- `conjugateEquiv_extendScalars_eqToHom`.
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
  apply Iso.ext
  rw [conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_hom]
  simp only [chartBaseChangeModuleReassocNat, chartBaseChangeRestrictReassocNat,
    eq_mpr_eq_cast, cast_eq, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, eqToIso.hom]
  set inclR := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR
  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR'
  set œÅB := chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ with hœÅB
  have hRing : œÅB.hom.comp inclR.hom = inclR'.hom.comp œÅ.hom := by
    rw [‚Üê CommRingCat.hom_comp, ‚Üê CommRingCat.hom_comp]
    exact congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (chartBaseChange_ring_square œà œÜ œÅ)
  set adjA := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÅ.hom).comp
    (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclR'.hom) with hadjA
  set adjB := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclR.hom).comp
    (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÅB.hom) with hadjB
  set adjMŒ± := ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (œÅB.hom.comp inclR.hom) with hadjMŒ±
  set adjMŒ≤ := ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj (inclR'.hom.comp œÅ.hom) with hadjMŒ≤
  -- Factor 3 (the `.inv` direction): invert `conjugateEquiv_extendScalarsComp`.
  have f3 : conjugateEquiv adjMŒ± adjB (ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).inv
      = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).hom := by
    set K := conjugateIsoEquiv adjMŒ± adjB (ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).symm
      with hKdef
    have hKhom : K.hom
        = conjugateEquiv adjMŒ± adjB (ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).inv := by
      rw [hKdef, conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
    have hKinv : K.inv = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).inv := by
      rw [hKdef, conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_inv, Iso.symm_inv]
      exact conjugateEquiv_extendScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom
    rw [‚Üê hKhom]
    calc K.hom
        = K.hom ‚â´ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).inv ‚â´
            (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).hom := by
          rw [Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
      _ = K.hom ‚â´ K.inv ‚â´ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).hom := by rw [‚Üê hKinv]
      _ = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp inclR.hom œÅB.hom).hom := by rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
  -- Split the 3-factor conjugate (right-associated) with `conjugateEquiv_comp` (twice).
  rw [‚Üê conjugateEquiv_comp adjA adjMŒ± adjB,
    ‚Üê conjugateEquiv_comp adjA adjMŒ≤ adjMŒ±,
    conjugateEquiv_extendScalarsComp, conjugateEquiv_extendScalars_eqToHom hRing, f3,
    Category.assoc]

/-- **Per-congruence mate coherence.** For parallel scheme morphisms `f = g`, the conjugate (adjoint
mate) of the pullback congruence iso `pullbackCongr hf` is the pushforward congruence iso
`pushforwardCongr hf`. The congruence piece (a single `eqToIso`, not a binary composite) of the
geometric leg `chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison_mate`. Project-local. -/
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackCongr {X Y : Scheme} {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) :
    conjugateEquiv (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g)
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hf).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hf).inv := by
  subst hf
  have hp : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (rfl : f = f)).hom = ùüô _ := by
    simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr (rfl : f = f)).inv = ùüô _ := by
    aesop_cat
  rw [hp, conjugateEquiv_id, hq]

/-- The inverse of a pushforward congruence iso is the forward map of the reversed congruence.
Reconciles the two orientations of the congruence square in
`chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison_mate`. Project-local. -/
lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_eq {X Y : Scheme} {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (hf : f = g) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hf).inv = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hf.symm).hom := by
  subst hf
  aesop_cat

/-- **Natural-iso form of the geometric base-change comparison on the pushforward side** ‚Äî the
right-adjoint mate target for `chartBaseChangeGeometricComparisonNat`. The composite pushforward
functor `pushforward (Spec Œπ_{R'}) ‚ãô pushforward (Spec œÅ)` is naturally isomorphic to
`pushforward (Spec œÅ_B) ‚ãô pushforward (Spec Œπ_R)` via the two `pushforwardComp` coherences and the
`pushforwardCongr` of the same base-change geometric square. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def chartBaseChangePushforwardNat {A R R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (œà : A ‚ü∂ B) (œÜ : A ‚ü∂ R) (œÅ : R ‚ü∂ R') :
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
          (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))))) ‚ãô
      Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map œÅ) ‚âÖ
      Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ)) ‚ãô
        Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
            (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))))) := by
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
  set inclR := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR
  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR'
  set œÅB := chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ with hœÅB
  have hsq : Spec.map inclR' ‚â´ Spec.map œÅ = Spec.map œÅB ‚â´ Spec.map inclR := by
    rw [‚Üê Spec.map_comp, ‚Üê Spec.map_comp]
    exact (congrArg Spec.map (chartBaseChange_ring_square œà œÜ œÅ)).symm
  exact (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map inclR') (Spec.map œÅ)) ‚â™‚â´
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hsq) ‚â™‚â´
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).symm

/-- **(b2) Geometric-leg coherence: the geometric comparison is the mate of the pushforward
coherence** (blueprint `lem:chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison_mate`). Under the composite
pullback‚Äìpushforward adjunctions, the conjugate (adjoint mate) of the geometric comparison
`chartBaseChangeGeometricComparisonNat` (built from `pullbackComp`) is the matching pushforward
comparison `chartBaseChangePushforwardNat` (built from `pushforwardComp`). This is the geometric leg
of the ring-square mate-glue `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue`. Project-local. -/
theorem chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison_mate {A R R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (œà : A ‚ü∂ B) (œÜ : A ‚ü∂ R) (œÅ : R ‚ü∂ R') :
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
    conjugateIsoEquiv
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÅ)).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
            (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
              (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u)))))))
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
            (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
              (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u)))))).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map (chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ))))
        (chartBaseChangeGeometricComparisonNat œà œÜ œÅ)
      = chartBaseChangePushforwardNat œà œÜ œÅ := by
  -- ROUTE (iter-018, analogist `fbc-geom-mate`, ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB): the per-factor mate fact
  -- `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` IS real Mathlib (`Sheaf.lean:238`, `@[simp]`); the iter-017
  -- "comment-fiction" note was FALSE. Drive entirely by closed coherence lemmas: split the 3-factor
  -- conjugate with `conjugateEquiv_comp` over named midpoint adjunctions, then discharge each factor by
  -- `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (√ó2, one inverted) + `conjugateEquiv_pullbackCongr`. No
  -- `unit_conjugateEquiv`-over-the-composite (that is the 200k-hb bomb).
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R : Type u) := œÜ.hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (R' : Type u) := (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := œà.hom.toAlgebra
  apply Iso.ext
  rw [conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_hom]
  simp only [chartBaseChangeGeometricComparisonNat, chartBaseChangePushforwardNat,
    eq_mpr_eq_cast, cast_eq, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
  set inclR := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR
  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
    (R := (A : Type u)) (A := (R' : Type u)) (B := (B : Type u))) with hinclR'
  set œÅB := chartBaseChangeRingMap œà œÜ œÅ with hœÅB
  -- Midpoint adjunctions for the two `conjugateEquiv_comp` splits.
  set adjA := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÅ)).comp
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclR')) with hadjA
  set adjB := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclR)).comp
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÅB)) with hadjB
  set adjMid1 := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÅB ‚â´ Spec.map inclR)
    with hadjMid1
  set adjMid2 := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclR' ‚â´ Spec.map œÅ)
    with hadjMid2
  -- Factor 3 (the `.hom` direction): obtained by inverting `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`.
  have f3 : conjugateEquiv adjMid1 adjB
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).hom
      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).inv := by
    have hk : (conjugateIsoEquiv adjMid1 adjB
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR))).inv
        = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).hom := by
      rw [conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_inv]
      exact conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)
    rw [‚Üê conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_hom]
    set K := conjugateIsoEquiv adjMid1 adjB
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)) with hK
    calc K.hom
        = K.hom ‚â´ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).hom ‚â´
            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).inv := by
          rw [Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
      _ = K.hom ‚â´ K.inv ‚â´ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).inv := by
          rw [‚Üê hk]
      _ = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (Spec.map œÅB) (Spec.map inclR)).inv := by
          rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
  -- Split the 3-factor conjugate (right-associated) with `conjugateEquiv_comp` (twice).
  rw [‚Üê conjugateEquiv_comp adjA adjMid1 adjB,
    ‚Üê conjugateEquiv_comp adjA adjMid2 adjMid1,
    conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, conjugateEquiv_pullbackCongr, f3,
    pushforwardCongr_inv_eq, Category.assoc]

/-! ### (b2 glue) Nat-trans-level decomposition of the iterated-mate assembly

The six lemmas below decompose the iterated-mate glue
`pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue` into nat-trans/TwoSquare-level steps, each closable

composite-adjunction unit to weak-head-normal form ‚Äî the documented kernel timeout). Steps 1‚Äì2 are
definitional whiskering lifts that fold a `.map`/`.app`-wrapped leg back into a whiskered nat-trans;
step 3 recognises a single dictionary leg as an iterated single-step mate; steps 4‚Äì5 wire the two

/-- **(b2 glue) Whisker-right lift of a pushforward-wrapped leg** (blueprint
`lem:ring_square_glue_whiskerRight_lift`). The image `H.map (Œ¶.app M)` of a natural-transformation

`whiskerRight Œ¶ H`. Definitional (Mathlib's `whiskerRight_app`, read right-to-left); folds a
`pushforward.map`-wrapped dictionary leg into a whiskered nat-trans so the composite unit stays
folded. Project-local. -/
lemma ring_square_glue_whiskerRight_lift {C D E : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Category E]
    {F G : C ‚•§ D} (Œ¶ : F ‚ü∂ G) (H : D ‚•§ E) (M : C) :
    H.map (Œ¶.app M) = (Functor.whiskerRight Œ¶ H).app M := rfl

/-- **(b2 glue) Whisker-left lift of an extend-scalars-wrapped leg** (blueprint
`lem:ring_square_glue_whiskerLeft_lift`). The component `Œ¶.app (F.obj M)` of a natural transformation
at a pre-composed functor's image is the `M`-component of the left-whiskered transformation
`whiskerLeft F Œ¶`. Definitional (Mathlib's `whiskerLeft_app`, read right-to-left); folds an
`extendScalars`-wrapped dictionary leg into a whiskered nat-trans. Project-local. -/
lemma ring_square_glue_whiskerLeft_lift {C D E : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Category E]
    (F : C ‚•§ D) {G H : D ‚•§ E} (Œ¶ : G ‚ü∂ H) (M : C) :
    Œ¶.app (F.obj M) = (Functor.whiskerLeft F Œ¶).app M := rfl

/-- **(b2 glue) Iterated single-step-mate recognition of one dictionary leg** (blueprint
`lem:ring_square_glue_pst_iterated_mate`). For a ring map `œÜ : R ‚ü∂ R'` and any TwoSquare `Œ±` of the
two single-step left adjoints, the conjugate of `Œ±` over the binary composite adjunctions
`adjL = (tilde ‚ä£ Œì).comp (pullback ‚ä£ pushforward)` and
`adjR = (extendScalars ‚ä£ restrictScalars).comp (tilde ‚ä£ Œì)` is the natural transformation underlying
the iterated single-step mate `mateEquiv (pullback ‚ä£ pushforward) (extendScalars ‚ä£ restrictScalars)
(mateEquiv (tilde ‚ä£ Œì) (tilde ‚ä£ Œì) Œ±)`. This is Mathlib's `iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv`
specialised to the affine-dictionary adjunctions, read in the displayed direction. It exposes the
iterated-mate structure of a dictionary leg without evaluating any component (the whole identity
is a structural TwoSquare-pasting equation), so the composite-adjunction unit is never forced to
whnf. Project-local. -/
lemma ring_square_glue_pst_iterated_mate {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (œÜ : R ‚ü∂ R')
    (Œ± : CategoryTheory.TwoSquare (ModuleCat.extendScalars œÜ.hom) (tilde.functor R)
      (tilde.functor R') (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÜ))) :
    (conjugateEquiv ((tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÜ)))
        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÜ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R')))) Œ±
      = ((mateEquiv (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÜ))
            (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÜ.hom))
          ((mateEquiv (tilde.adjunction (R := R)) (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))) Œ±)).natTrans :=
  (iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv (tilde.adjunction (R := R)) (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))

/-! ### (b2 glue) Iso-level conjugate telescoping ‚Äî carrier-free primitives for the cocycle

The blueprint cocycle `lem:ring_square_cocycle` telescopes the four dictionary legs (each a
`(conjugateIsoEquiv ‚Ä¶).symm Œ≥`) into a single conjugate of a composite, then "re-upgrades to
isomorphisms by `conjugateIsoEquiv`". Mathlib provides the *morphism*-level conjugate composition
laws `conjugateEquiv_comp` / `conjugateEquiv_symm_comp` but NOT their `Iso`-level companions. The two
lemmas below supply exactly those companions. They are pure category theory over abstract categories
‚Äî no sheafification / pullback / extend-scalars carrier occurs ‚Äî so no kernel weak-head-normal-form
reduction can be triggered (the documented `.app (tilde M)` kernel timeout). Project-local. -/

/-- **Iso-level conjugate composition** (blueprint feeds `lem:ring_square_cocycle`). The `Iso`-level
companion of Mathlib's morphism-level `conjugateEquiv_comp`: conjugating a composite of left-adjoint
isomorphisms is the (contravariant) composite of the conjugates. Carrier-free. -/
theorem conjugateIsoEquiv_comp {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
    {L‚ÇÅ L‚ÇÇ L‚ÇÉ : C ‚•§ D} {R‚ÇÅ R‚ÇÇ R‚ÇÉ : D ‚•§ C}
    (adj‚ÇÅ : L‚ÇÅ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÅ) (adj‚ÇÇ : L‚ÇÇ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÇ) (adj‚ÇÉ : L‚ÇÉ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÉ)
    (Œ± : L‚ÇÇ ‚âÖ L‚ÇÅ) (Œ≤ : L‚ÇÉ ‚âÖ L‚ÇÇ) :
    conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÇ Œ± ‚â™‚â´ conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÇ adj‚ÇÉ Œ≤
      = conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÉ (Œ≤ ‚â™‚â´ Œ±) := by
  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, conjugateIsoEquiv_apply_hom]
  rw [conjugateEquiv_comp]

/-- **Iso-level inverse-conjugate composition** (blueprint feeds `lem:ring_square_cocycle`). The
`Iso`-level companion of Mathlib's `conjugateEquiv_symm_comp`: the inverse conjugate of a composite
of right-adjoint isomorphisms is the (contravariant) composite of the inverse conjugates. This is the
form consumed by the four-leg telescope, since every dictionary leg `pullback_spec_tilde_iso œÜ` is
`((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso œÜ)).symm`. Carrier-free. -/
theorem conjugateIsoEquiv_symm_comp {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
    {L‚ÇÅ L‚ÇÇ L‚ÇÉ : C ‚•§ D} {R‚ÇÅ R‚ÇÇ R‚ÇÉ : D ‚•§ C}
    (adj‚ÇÅ : L‚ÇÅ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÅ) (adj‚ÇÇ : L‚ÇÇ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÇ) (adj‚ÇÉ : L‚ÇÉ ‚ä£ R‚ÇÉ)
    (Œ± : R‚ÇÅ ‚âÖ R‚ÇÇ) (Œ≤ : R‚ÇÇ ‚âÖ R‚ÇÉ) :
    (conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÇ adj‚ÇÉ).symm Œ≤ ‚â™‚â´ (conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÇ).symm Œ±
      = (conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÉ).symm (Œ± ‚â™‚â´ Œ≤) := by
  rw [Equiv.eq_symm_apply,
    ‚Üê conjugateIsoEquiv_comp adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÇ adj‚ÇÉ
      ((conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÅ adj‚ÇÇ).symm Œ±) ((conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚ÇÇ adj‚ÇÉ).symm Œ≤),
    Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]

/-- **Functor-level affine pullback dictionary.** The natural isomorphism of functors
`ModuleCat R ‚•§ (Spec R').Modules`
\[ \widetilde{(-)} \;\circ\; \operatorname{pullback}(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)
   \;\cong\; \operatorname{extendScalars}\varphi \;\circ\; \widetilde{(-)} \]
whose `M`-component is `pullback_spec_tilde_iso œÜ M`. This is exactly the conjugate (via
`conjugateIsoEquiv`) of `gammaPushforwardNatIso œÜ`, repackaged at the functor level so the four-leg

-/

    tilde.functor R ‚ãô Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map œÜ) ‚âÖ
      ModuleCat.extendScalars œÜ.hom ‚ãô tilde.functor R' :=
  ((conjugateIsoEquiv ((tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map œÜ)))
      ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj œÜ.hom).comp
        (tilde.adjunction (R := R')))).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso œÜ)).symm

/-- The `M`-component of `pullbackSpecTildeNatIso œÜ` is `pullback_spec_tilde_iso œÜ M`, definitionally

/-- **The section-level base-change map is the base change of the unit.** With the domain and
codomain pinned by `base_change_mate_domain_read` (`Œò_src`) and `base_change_mate_codomain_read`

`lem:base_change_mate_section_identity`.

The argument is carried out directly on the module of global sections; no abstract adjoint-mate
identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By `ModuleCat` hom-extensionality it suffices to
check on the `R'`-module generators `1 ‚äó‚Çú x` (`ext x`), leaving the concrete per-generator identity
`(Œò_src‚Åª¬π ‚â´ Œì(Œ∏) ‚â´ Œò_tgt) (1 ‚äó x) = regroupEquiv.inv (1 ‚äó x)`. By construction `Œì(Œ∏)` is, read on
global sections through the two tilde dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` /
`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` packaged in `Œò_src`, `Œò_tgt`, the extension of scalars along `œà : R ‚Üí R'`
of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along `A ‚Üí A ‚äó_R R'` (`m ‚Ü¶ (1 ‚äó 1) ‚äó m`); evaluating
through the `R'`-action on the target gives `1 ‚äó x ‚Ü¶ (1 ‚äó 1) ‚äó x`, which is exactly
`regroupEquiv.inv` on generators (both sides `R'`-linear). This theorem's **body has no inline

but it is **transitively `sorry`-backed** through `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. The single

of the whole Seam-3 chain ‚Äî the mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square ‚Äî lives in

theorem base_change_mate_section_identity {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (œà : R ‚ü∂ R') (œÜ : R ‚ü∂ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    (base_change_mate_domain_read œà œÜ M).inv ‚â´
      (moduleSpecŒìFunctor (R := R')).map
        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)
          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)).w (tilde M)) ‚â´
      (base_change_mate_codomain_read œà œÜ M).hom
      = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv œà œÜ M).inv := by
  -- COUNIT FACTORIZATION (blueprint Seam 3 step): `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is by definition the
  -- `(g^* ‚ä£ g_*)`-adjunction transpose `((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm inner`
  -- of the inner pushforward comparison `inner`. By `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit` this transpose is
  -- `g^*(inner) ‚â´ counit`, with no opaque adjunction transpose remaining. After this rewrite the goal
  -- is exactly the statement of Seam 3 (`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`).
  unfold pushforwardBaseChangeMap
  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_counit]
  exact base_change_mate_gstar_transpose œà œÜ M

/-- **Generator trace of the section-level base-change map.** With the domain and codomain pinned
by `base_change_mate_domain_read` (`Œò_src`) and `base_change_mate_codomain_read` (`Œò_tgt`), the

`r' ‚äó m ‚Ü¶ (1 ‚äó r') ‚äó m`, i.e. it is `cancelBaseChange‚Åª¬π` (after the `A ‚äó_R R' ‚âÖ R' ‚äó_R A`
regrouping); since `cancelBaseChange` is a Mathlib `LinearEquiv` with no flatness hypothesis, the
conjugate is an isomorphism. This `IsIso` form is the iso-consequence the affine close consumes
(mirroring the parent `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`); the proof obligation is the
adjoint-mate unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` on global sections through the two proved
tilde dictionaries. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace`. -/
theorem base_change_mate_generator_trace {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (œà : R ‚ü∂ R') (œÜ : R ‚ü∂ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    IsIso ((base_change_mate_domain_read œà œÜ M).inv ‚â´
      (moduleSpecŒìFunctor (R := R')).map
        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)
          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)).w (tilde M)) ‚â´
      (base_change_mate_codomain_read œà œÜ M).hom) := by
  -- By the section identity `base_change_mate_section_identity` the conjugated map equals
  -- `(base_change_mate_regroupEquiv œà œÜ M).inv`, the inverse of a `ModuleCat` isomorphism, hence an

/-! ## Section-level value of the affine base-change map -/

/-- **Section-level value of the affine base-change map.** In the affine‚Äìaffine model ‚Äî base ring
maps `œà : R ‚ü∂ R'` (the base change `g = Spec œà`) and `œÜ : R ‚ü∂ A` (the affine morphism
`f = Spec œÜ`), with `F = M^~` for an `A`-module `M`, the canonical pullback square supplying
`g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd` ‚Äî the global-sections incarnation `Œì(Œ±)` of the
base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism of `R'`-modules.

By the four affine dictionaries the domain `Œì(g^*(f_* M^~))` reads as `R' ‚äó_R M` and the codomain
`Œì(f'_*(g')^* M^~)` reads as `(R' ‚äó_R A) ‚äó_A M`, under which `Œì(Œ±)` is the canonical cancellation
isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
`Œì(Œ±) = cancelBaseChange‚Åª¬π`, `r' ‚äó m ‚Ü¶ (r' ‚äó 1) ‚äó m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no
flatness hypothesis, `Œì(Œ±)` is an isomorphism. This is the section-level content of the affine
close: it is the per-affine-chart input to `base_change_map_affine_local` once the locality
reduction has restricted an arbitrary square to this affine‚Äìaffine model. See blueprint
`lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`.

The proof is the 4-step generator trace of the blueprint: unwind the adjoint mate of the
`((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit on global sections through `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and
`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`, landing on `cancelBaseChange‚Åª¬π`. This theorem's **body has no inline
`sorry`** (it conjugates `base_change_mate_generator_trace` back through the domain/codomain reads),
but it is **transitively `sorry`-backed** through `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. The genuine
crux ‚Äî the mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square
`pullback (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)` ‚Äî is the outstanding obligation carried by
`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (and, beneath it, `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`), NOT
here. -/
theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange
    {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (œà : R ‚ü∂ R') (œÜ : R ‚ü∂ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    IsIso ((moduleSpecŒìFunctor (R := R')).map
      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)
        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
        (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)).w (tilde M))) := by
  -- Assemble the chain (blueprint `lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`): the domain
  -- read `Œò_src` (`base_change_mate_domain_read`) and codomain read `Œò_tgt`
  -- (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, resting on the `pullbackSpecIso` leg identification
  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`) identify `Œì(Œ±)`'s domain/codomain with `R' ‚äó_R M` and
  -- `(A ‚äó_R R') ‚äó_A M`; the generator trace `base_change_mate_generator_trace` shows the conjugate
  -- `Œò_src‚Åª¬π ‚â´ Œì(Œ±) ‚â´ Œò_tgt` is an isomorphism (it is `cancelBaseChange‚Åª¬π`, no flatness).
  -- Conjugating back, `Œì(Œ±)` is itself an isomorphism.
  haveI hconj := base_change_mate_generator_trace œà œÜ M
  set D := base_change_mate_domain_read œà œÜ M with hD
  set C := base_change_mate_codomain_read œà œÜ M with hC
  set ŒìŒ± := (moduleSpecŒìFunctor (R := R')).map
    (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)
      (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
      (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà))
      (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map œÜ) (Spec.map œà)).w (tilde M)) with hŒìŒ±
  have heq : ŒìŒ± = D.hom ‚â´ (D.inv ‚â´ ŒìŒ± ‚â´ C.hom) ‚â´ C.inv := by
    simp [Category.assoc]
  rw [heq]
  infer_instance

/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/

/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine‚Äìaffine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (H : ‚àÄ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H

/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
`(R' ‚äó_R A) ‚äó_A M ‚âÖ R' ‚äó_R M`, which needs no flatness.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine‚Äìaffine
  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
  intro U
  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap ‚Ä¶) U)` for `U` affine.
  --
  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
  --   ‚Ä¢ the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
  --     (above): in the affine‚Äìaffine model `Œì(Œ±) = cancelBaseChange‚Åª¬π`, hence an iso (no
  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
  --   ‚Ä¢ the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
  --
  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ‚äÜ S'` and a chosen affine
  -- `Spec R ‚äÜ S` containing `g(U)` ‚Äî over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` ‚Äî and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
  -- ‚Ä¶).app U` with the affine‚Äìaffine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the
  -- tilde‚ÄìŒì counit isos to pass `IsIso Œì(Œ±) ‚üπ IsIso Œ±` on the QC chart). This restriction-
  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining
  -- multi-hundred-LOC build for the unconditional general theorem.
  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
  sorry

/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
